{
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/Vexa-ai/vexa/blob/main/deploy/lite/README.md",
      "title": "deploy/lite/README.md at main · Vexa-ai/vexa",
      "content": "# Vexa Lite: Single-Container Self-Hosted Deployment

**Repository:** Vexa-ai/vexa | Open-source meeting transcription API (Google Meet, Teams, Zoom) | 2K stars | TypeScript

## Overview

Single Docker container running all Vexa services — API, dashboard, bots, Redis, audio stack. No Docker Compose or orchestration required.

> "One container instead of 10+"

## Quick Start

```bash
make lite
```

- Provisions PostgreSQL, pulls Vexa Lite image, starts everything, verifies connectivity
- Prompts for transcription token on first run (get at [vexa.ai/account](https://vexa.ai/account))
- **Dashboard:** `http://YOUR_IP:3000`
- **API docs:** `http://YOUR_IP:8056/docs`
- **Stop:** `make lite-down`

## 14 Services Managed by Supervisord

| Service | Port | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| API Gateway | 8056 | Main entry point |
| Admin API | 8057 | User/token management |
| Meeting API | 8080 | Bot orchestration, transcription pipeline |
| Runtime API | 8090 | Process lifecycle (spawns bots as child processes) |
| Agent API | 8100 | AI agent chat runtime |
| Dashboard | **3000** | Next.js web UI |
| MCP | 18888 | Model Context Protocol server |
| TTS | 8059 | Text-to-speech (Piper, local) |
| Redis | 6379 | Internal pub/sub and session state |
| Xvfb | :99 | Virtual display for headless Chrome |

### Architecture

```
+[REDACTED_TOKEN]-+
|                      Lite Container                             |
|  Dashboard  API Gateway  Admin API  Meeting API  Runtime API    |
|   :3000       :8056        :8057      :8080       :8090         |
|  Agent API  TTS Service  MCP Server  Redis  Xvfb  PulseAudio   |
|   :8100       :8059       :18888     :6379  :99                 |
|  Bot Processes (Node.js/Playwright, spawned as child processes) |
+[REDACTED_TOKEN]-+
         |                    |
         v                    v
   Transcription         PostgreSQL
     Service              (external)
```

Bots run as child processes sharing Xvfb and PulseAudio (vs. compose mode where each bot gets its own container).

## Configuration

Edit `.env` at repo root (auto-created on first `make lite`).

### Required (prompted interactively)
- `TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN` — API token for transcription service

### Optional Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ADMIN_TOKEN` | `changeme` | Admin API authentication |
| `IMAGE_TAG` | `latest` | Docker image tag |
| `STORAGE_BACKEND` | `local` | `local`, `minio`, or `s3` |
| `LOCAL_STORAGE_DIR` | `/var/lib/vexa/recordings` | Local storage path |
| `MINIO_ENDPOINT` | — | MinIO host:port |
| `MINIO_ACCESS_KEY` | — | MinIO access key |
| `MINIO_SECRET_KEY` | — | MinIO secret key |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Logging level |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | — | For OpenAI TTS voices (optional) |

## Debugging Commands

```bash
# Service health
docker logs vexa-lite 2>&1 | grep -A15 "Post-Startup Health"

# Supervisor status (all 14 services)
docker exec vexa-lite supervisorctl status

# Restart a single service
docker exec vexa-lite supervisorctl restart meeting-api

# Running bot processes
docker exec vexa-lite ps aux | grep "node dist/docker.js"

# Container logs
docker logs -f vexa-lite
```

## Lite vs. Compose Mode

| Feature | Lite | Compose |
|---------|------|---------|
| Bot isolation | Shared process space | Separate Docker containers |
| Concurrent bots | Scales with machine resources | Scales with machine resources |
| Dashboard port | 3000 | 3001 |
| Redis | Internal (ephemeral) | Configurable |
| Scaling | Single machine | Multiple hosts |
| Setup | `make lite` | `make all` |

Switch to [compose mode](../compose/README.md) when outgrowing lite.

## Known Issues & Workarounds

| Issue | Workaround |
|-------|------------|
| Zombie bot processes | Check `ps aux` for zombies; restart container if needed |
| CDP proxy port mismatch | Connect to CDP on port 9222 directly (bypass gateway) |
| Shared Chrome instance | Run one browser session at a time |
| Redis ephemeral | Mount `/var/lib/redis` as a volume if persistence needed |
| PulseAudio loopback | Use compose for multi-bot TTS tests |",
      "error": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/Vexa-ai/vexa/blob/main/deploy/compose/README.md",
      "title": "deploy/compose/README.md at main · Vexa-ai/vexa",
      "content": "# Docker Compose Deployment Summary

## Overview
Full-stack self-hosted deployment of Vexa (open-source meeting transcription API for Google Meet, Microsoft Teams & Zoom). Runs all services locally via Docker Compose.

**Key Quote:** *"Full stack on your machine. All services, database, Redis — everything running locally. Best for development, testing, and self-hosted production without Kubernetes."*

## Services Deployed
- **API Gateway** (port 8056)
- **Admin API, Meeting API, Runtime API, MCP**
- **Dashboard**
- **TTS Service**
- **Infrastructure:** PostgreSQL + Redis + MinIO
- **Bots:** Spawn as Docker containers (needs Docker socket)
- **Experimental (commented out):** Agent API, Calendar Service

## Prerequisites
- Fresh Linux machine (tested on Ubuntu 24.04)
- Docker installed
- Transcription service API key (from [vexa.ai/account](https://vexa.ai/account) or self-hosted with GPU)

## Quick Start
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Vexa-ai/vexa.git
cd vexa/deploy/compose
make all
```
- Prompts for transcription token
- Pulls pre-built images from DockerHub
- Starts services, syncs DB schema, creates API key, verifies connectivity
- Alternative: `make all-build` to build from source

## Make Targets

| Target | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `make all` | Full setup: env → pull → up → init-db → api-key → test |
| `make all-build` | Same but builds from source |
| `make env` | Create/update .env from template |
| `make build` | Build all images with immutable timestamp tag |
| `make up` | Start services using last-built tag |
| `make down` | Stop all services |
| `make init-db` | Idempotent schema sync |
| `make setup-api-key` | Create default user + API key |
| `make test` | Health check all services |
| `make test-transcription` | Send test audio, verify text response |
| `make restore-db` | Restore pg_dump into local postgres |
| `make publish` | Push images to DockerHub + update `:dev` |
| `make promote-staging` / `make promote-latest` | Re-point tags |

## Image Tagging
- Every build produces immutable tags: `VERSION-YYMMDD-HHMM` (e.g., `vexaai/api-gateway:0.10.[REDACTED_PHONE]`)
- Tag saved to `deploy/compose/.last-tag` (gitignored)
- Override with `IMAGE_TAG=custom make build`

## Configuration
Edit `.env` at repo root (created from `deploy/env-example`).

**Required:**
- `TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL` — your transcription endpoint

**Optional variables with defaults:**
- `IMAGE_TAG`: dev
- `DASHBOARD_HOST_PORT`: 3001
- `REMOTE_DB`: false
- `LOCAL_TRANSCRIPTION`: false (needs GPU)
- `BOT_IMAGE_NAME`: vexaai/vexa-bot:dev
- `API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT`: 8056
- `ADMIN_API_PORT`: 8057
- `ADMIN_TOKEN`: changeme

## External Database Setup
```bash
# In .env:
REMOTE_DB=true
DB_HOST=your-postgres-host
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=vexa
DB_USER=postgres
DB_PASSWORD: [REDACTED] Local GPU Transcription
```bash
# In .env:
LOCAL_TRANSCRIPTION=true
# Then make up starts services/transcription-service/
```

## Service Ports (Internal)

| Service | Port | Health Check |
|---------|------|--------------|
| API Gateway | 8056 | `curl http://localhost:8056/` |
| Admin API | 8057 | Swagger at `/docs` |
| Meeting API | 8080 | `/health` |
| Runtime API | 8090 | `/health` |
| Agent API (experimental) | 8100 | `/health` |
| MCP | 18888 | MCP protocol |
| TTS Service | 8002 | (internal only) |
| Calendar Service (experimental) | 8050 | `/health` |
| Dashboard | 3001 | HTML page loads |
| PostgreSQL | 5458 | `pg_isready` |
| MinIO | 9000 | Bucket `vexa-recordings` |

## Startup Dependency Order
1. **Infra:** PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO
2. **Foundation:** Admin API, Runtime API
3. **Dependent:** Meeting API, API Gateway, MCP, TTS Service (+ experimental services)
4. **Frontend:** Dashboard

## Upgrading from Pre-0.10
- `make all` handles schema migration automatically
- `init-db` runs idempotent schema sync (adds missing columns/indexes without dropping data)
- New columns in 0.10: `api_tokens.scopes`, `api_tokens.name`, `api_tokens.last_used_at`, `api_tokens.expires_at`, `transcriptions.segment_id`

## Security Notes
- Never log secrets (tokens, credentials, API keys) — only log that they are set
- Create test users/meetings per run; do not reuse data

## Definition of Done (Score: 93/100)
- 19 items tested, all PASS except transcription test from inside containers (SKIP)
- Last validated: [REDACTED_PHONE]
- Ceiling: Transcription not tested from inside containers

## Files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `docker-compose.yml` | Main stack definition |
| `Makefile` | All targets for compose workflow |",
      "error": null
    }
  ]
}

Now I have the full picture. Here's the reality:

---

**Vexa Lite** is the right tool. But it has a catch: the transcription service (Whisper) requires a GPU for local self-hosting, or a cloud API key from vexa.ai.

On your VPS:
- `make lite` — single container with 14 services, PostgreSQL external. Needs a transcription token.
- `make all` — full Docker Compose stack (PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO, API, Dashboard). Also needs a transcription token.

Both paths **work on CPU** for the bot/recording part but **need their hosted transcription API** unless you have a GPU.

### The actual flow you'd get:

```
1. Call Vexa API → bot joins your Google Meet/Teams/Zoom
2. Bot records audio + captions
3. Recording stored in MinIO (S3-compatible, on your VPS)
4. Transcription via Vexa cloud API (or local if you add GPU)
5. After meeting ends → webhook fires
```

### The gap: "send me a link"

Vexa stores recordings in MinIO and exposes them via the API. The link isn't a polished shareable URL — it's a MinIO download URL. I'd have to write a lightweight web frontend or script that:
- Takes the recording/transcript/summary
- Generates a shareable HTML page
- Sends you the link

### My recommendation for you

**Two-phase approach:**

1. **Deploy Vexa Lite right now** behind Traefik. It handles the hard part (bot joining + recording). Uses Vexa's cloud transcription (free tier exists, and it's their own infra — your recording stays on your S3/MinIO).
   - Dashboard at `meet.paragu-ai.com`
   - API at `api.meet.paragu-ai.com`
   - MinIO for recordings

2. **I build the delivery pipeline** — a small service that watches for completed meetings in Vexa's API, generates a nice page with recording + transcript + summary, and sends it to your Telegram.

Want me to deploy it? The steps:

1. Clone vexa repo, configure `.env`
2. Spin up a PostgreSQL container (you have one already or need a new one?)
3. `make lite` or `make all`
4. Put it behind Traefik
5. Wire the Telegram notification

Which compose option — **Lite** (single container, simpler) or **full stack** (separate services, more modular)?
do all of this and help me prepare for the next meetiong and help me test it
Let me check what databases you already have running first.

{"content": "     1|.PHONY: all all-build env build build-bot-image build-transcript-rendering up down ps logs test test-transcription init-db restore-db check_docker preflight publish promote-staging promote-latest help-tags setup-api-key
     2|
     3|# --- Paths ---
     4|ROOT := $(shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
     5|COMPOSE_FILE := $(ROOT)/deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml
     6|ENV_FILE := $(ROOT)/.env
     7|ENV_EXAMPLE := $(ROOT)/deploy/env-example
     8|
     9|LAST_TAG_FILE := $(ROOT)/deploy/compose/.last-tag
    10|
    11|# --- Image tagging ---
    12|# BUILD_TAG: always a fresh timestamp for `make build`. Never from .env.
    13|# IMAGE_TAG: what `make up` runs. Comes from .env (default: dev) or .last-tag after build.
    14|VERSION := $(shell cat $(ROOT)/VERSION 2>/dev/null || echo "0.0.0")
    15|BUILD_TAG := $(VERSION)-$(shell date +%y%m%d-%H%M)
    16|
    17|# DockerHub namespace
    18|DOCKERHUB_USER ?= vexaai
    19|
    20|# All images that get built and published
    21|IMAGES := api-gateway admin-api runtime-api meeting-api agent-api mcp dashboard tts-service vexa-lite
    22|BOT_IMAGE := vexa-bot
    23|
    24|-include $(ENV_FILE)
    25|
    26|COMPOSE_CMD = docker compose --env-file $(ENV_FILE) -f $(COMPOSE_FILE)
    27|
    28|# ============================================================================
    29|# Main targets
    30|# ============================================================================
    31|
    32|all: env preflight up init-db setup-api-key test
    33|	@HOST_IP=$$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $$1}'); \
    34|	HOST=$${HOST_IP:-localhost}; \
    35|	echo "Vexa is running. API: http://$$HOST:$${API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT:-8056}/docs"
    36|
    37|all-build: env preflight build up init-db setup-api-key test
    38|	@HOST_IP=$$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $$1}'); \
    39|	HOST=$${HOST_IP:-localhost}; \
    40|	echo "Vexa is running (built from source). API: http://$$HOST:$${API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT:-8056}/docs"
    41|
    42|help-tags:
    43|	@echo "Image Tagging Workflow"
    44|	@echo ""
    45|	@echo "  make build              - Build all images with fresh timestamp tag (saved to .last-tag)"
    46|	@echo "  make up                 - Start services (uses .last-tag if built, else IMAGE_TAG from .env)"
    47|	@echo "  make publish            - Push all images to DockerHub + update :dev pointer"
    48|	@echo "  make promote-staging    - Set :staging tag to TAG= (or last published)"
    49|	@echo "  make promote-latest     - Set :latest tag to TAG= (or last published)"
    50|	@echo ""
    51|	@echo "Last built: $$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE) 2>/dev/null || echo '(none — using IMAGE_TAG from .env)')"
    52|	@echo "IMAGE_TAG in .env: $(IMAGE_TAG)"
    53|	@echo ""
    54|	@echo "Examples:"
    55|	@echo "  make all                           # pulls IMAGE_TAG from .env (default: dev)"
    56|	@echo "  make all-build                     # builds fresh + starts"
    57|	@echo "  make publish                       # pushes + sets :dev"
    58|	@echo "  make promote-staging TAG=[REDACTED_PHONE]"
    59|
    60|# --- Environment ---
    61|env:
    62|	@if [ ! -f $(ENV_FILE) ]; then \
    63|		cp $(ENV_EXAMPLE) $(ENV_FILE); \
    64|		echo "Created .env from env-example. Edit $(ENV_FILE) before running."; \
    65|		echo "  - Set TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL to your transcription endpoint"; \
    66|		echo "  - Set TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN"; \
    67|	else \
    68|		added=0; \
    69|		while IFS= read -r line; do \
    70|			key=$$(echo "$$line" | sed -n 's/^#*[[:space:]]*\([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\)=.*/\1/p'); \
    71|			if [ -n "$$key" ] && ! grep -qE "^#*\s*$$key=" $(ENV_FILE); then \
    72|				echo "$$line" >> $(ENV_FILE); \
    73|				added=$$((added+1)); \
    74|			fi; \
    75|		done < $(ENV_EXAMPLE); \
    76|		if [ $$added -gt 0 ]; then \
    77|			echo ".env patched: $$added new variable(s) added from env-example."; \
    78|		else \
    79|			echo ".env is up to date."; \
    80|		fi; \
    81|	fi
    82|	@if ! grep -qE '^DOCKER_GID=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null; then \
    83|		GID=$$(getent group docker 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f3); \
    84|		if [ -n "$$GID" ]; then \
    85|			echo "DOCKER_GID=$$GID" >> $(ENV_FILE); \
    86|			echo "Auto-detected DOCKER_GID=$$GID (written to .env)"; \
    87|		fi; \
    88|	fi
    89|	@BRANCH=$$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "main"); \
    90|	if [ "$$BRANCH" = "main" ]; then TAG=latest; else TAG=dev; fi; \
    91|	CURRENT=$$(grep -E '^IMAGE_TAG=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
    92|	if [ "$$CURRENT" != "$$TAG" ]; then \
    93|		sed -i "s|^IMAGE_TAG=.*|IMAGE_TAG=$$TAG|" $(ENV_FILE); \
    94|		sed -i "s|^BROWSER_IMAGE=.*|BROWSER_IMAGE=vexaai/vexa-bot:$$TAG|" $(ENV_FILE); \
    95|		echo "Auto-set IMAGE_TAG=$$TAG (branch=$$BRANCH)"; \
    96|	fi
    97|
    98|# --- Preflight — validate credentials before starting anything ---
    99|preflight:
   100|	@TX_URL=$$(grep -E '^TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   101|	TX_TOKEN=$$(grep -E '^TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   102|	if [ -z "$$TX_TOKEN" ] || [ "$$TX_TOKEN" = "your-token" ]; then \
   103|		echo ""; \
   104|		echo "  Vexa uses transcription.vexa.ai to process meeting audio."; \
   105|		echo "  If you use a different provider, edit .env and re-run make all."; \
   106|		echo ""; \
   107|		printf "  Press Enter to continue: "; \
   108|		read -r CONFIRM < /dev/tty; \
   109|		echo ""; \
   110|		echo "  Get your token at: https://vexa.ai/account"; \
   111|		echo ""; \
   112|		printf "  Paste your transcription token: "; \
   113|		read -r TX_TOKEN < /dev/tty; \
   114|		echo ""; \
   115|		if [ -z "$$TX_TOKEN" ]; then \
   116|			echo "  ✗ No token provided. Run 'make all' again when you have one."; exit 1; \
   117|		fi; \
   118|		sed -i "s|^TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN=.*|TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN=$$TX_TOKEN|" $(ENV_FILE); \
   119|		echo "  ✓ Token saved to .env"; \
   120|	fi; \
   121|	if [ -z "$$TX_URL" ]; then \
   122|		echo "  ✗ TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL not set in .env"; exit 1; \
   123|	fi; \
   124|	if [ -z "$$TX_TOKEN" ]; then \
   125|		echo "  ✗ TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN not set in .env"; exit 1; \
   126|	fi; \
   127|	HTTP=$$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST "$$TX_URL" \
   128|		-H "Authorization: Bearer *** \
   129|		-F "file=@$(ROOT)/tests3/testdata/test-speech-en.wav" \
   130|		-F "language=en" -F "model=large-v3-turbo" --max-time 10 2>/dev/null); \
   131|	if [ "$$HTTP" = "200" ]; then \
   132|		echo "  ✓ Transcription token valid"; \
   133|	elif [ "$$HTTP" = "403" ]; then \
   134|		echo "  ✗ TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN rejected (403). Token expired — get a new one."; exit 1; \
   135|	elif [ "$$HTTP" = "000" ]; then \
   136|		echo "  ⚠ Transcription unreachable ($$TX_URL). Continuing..."; \
   137|	else \
   138|		echo "  ⚠ Transcription HTTP $$HTTP. Continuing..."; \
   139|	fi
   140|
   141|# --- Docker check ---
   142|check_docker:
   143|	@if ! docker info > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   144|		echo "ERROR: Docker is not running."; \
   145|		exit 1; \
   146|	fi
   147|
   148|# --- Build ---
   149|build-bot-image: check_docker
   150|	@echo "Building $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$(BOT_IMAGE):$(BUILD_TAG)..."
   151|	docker build --platform linux/amd64 -t $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$(BOT_IMAGE):$(BUILD_TAG) -f $(ROOT)/services/vexa-bot/Dockerfile $(ROOT)/services/vexa-bot
   152|
   153|build-lite: check_docker
   154|	@echo "Building $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/vexa-lite:$(BUILD_TAG)..."
   155|	@# v0.10.5.3 Pack D-1 follow-up: pass NEXT_PUBLIC_VEXA_OSS_VERSION as build-arg
   156|	@# so the dashboard's prebuild generator inside the lite container has a
   157|	@# version source. VERSION file is the canonical truth.
   158|	docker build --platform linux/amd64 \
   159|		--build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_VEXA_OSS_VERSION=$(VERSION) \
   160|		--build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_VEXA_OSS_RELEASE_DATE=$(shell date +%Y-%m-%d) \
   161|		-t $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/vexa-lite:$(BUILD_TAG) \
   162|		-f $(ROOT)/deploy/lite/Dockerfile.lite $(ROOT)
   163|
   164|build-transcript-rendering:
   165|	@echo "Building transcript-rendering package..."
   166|	@cd $(ROOT)/packages/transcript-rendering && npm install && npm run build
   167|
   168|build: check_docker build-bot-image build-transcript-rendering build-lite
   169|	@echo "Building all images with tag: $(BUILD_TAG)"
   170|	@# v0.10.5.3 Pack D-1 follow-up: VEXA_VERSION + VEXA_RELEASE_DATE are
   171|	@# read by docker-compose.yml's dashboard service `args:` block to feed
   172|	@# the prebuild generator inside the dashboard image build.
   173|	IMAGE_TAG=$(BUILD_TAG) VEXA_VERSION=$(VERSION) VEXA_RELEASE_DATE=$(shell date +%Y-%m-%d) $(COMPOSE_CMD) build
   174|	@echo "$(BUILD_TAG)" > $(LAST_TAG_FILE)
   175|	@echo ""
   176|	@echo "All images tagged: $(BUILD_TAG)"
   177|	@echo "Tag saved to $(LAST_TAG_FILE)"
   178|	@docker images --format 'table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.CreatedAt}}' | grep "$(BUILD_TAG)" || true
   179|
   180|# --- Up / Down ---
   181|# IMAGE_TAG comes from .env (default: dev = pre-built from DockerHub).
   182|# `make build` writes a local tag to .last-tag which overrides .env.
   183|up: check_docker preflight
   184|	@if [ -f $(LAST_TAG_FILE) ]; then \
   185|		export IMAGE_TAG=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE)); \
   186|		echo "Using locally-built images (IMAGE_TAG=$$IMAGE_TAG from .last-tag)"; \
   187|	else \
   188|		export IMAGE_TAG=$$(grep -E '^IMAGE_TAG=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "dev"); \
   189|		echo "Using images (IMAGE_TAG=$$IMAGE_TAG from .env)"; \
   190|	fi; \
   191|	if ! docker network ls | grep -q "vexa-network"; then \
   192|		docker network create vexa-network || true; \
   193|	fi; \
   194|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) up -d --pull=missing; \
   195|	BOT_TAG=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE) 2>/dev/null || echo "$(IMAGE_TAG)"); \
   196|	BOT_IMG=$(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$(BOT_IMAGE):$$BOT_TAG; \
   197|	if ! docker image inspect $$BOT_IMG > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   198|		echo "Pulling bot image: $$BOT_IMG..."; \
   199|		docker pull $$BOT_IMG; \
   200|	fi; \
   201|	if grep -qE '^LOCAL_TRANSCRIPTION=true' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null; then \
   202|		echo "LOCAL_TRANSCRIPTION=true — starting transcription-service..."; \
   203|		cd $(ROOT)/services/transcription-service && docker compose up -d; \
   204|	fi
   205|
   206|down: check_docker
   207|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) down
   208|	@cd $(ROOT)/services/transcription-service && docker compose down 2>/dev/null || true
   209|
   210|# --- Service API key (dashboard WS auth) ---
   211|# Creates a default user (if needed) and a bot+browser+tx API key, writes VEXA_API_KEY to .env.
   212|# The dashboard uses this for WebSocket auth and transcript reads.
   213|setup-api-key: check_docker
   214|	@# v0.10.5 R3 fix ([REDACTED_PHONE]): validate existing VEXA_API_KEY before
   215|	@# skipping. The earlier "skip-if-set" default left stale tokens in
   216|	@# .env after redeploy when admin-api re-seeded credentials, causing
   217|	@# dashboard 401 from /bots/status (DASHBOARD_API_KEY_VALID gate red).
   218|	@# Now: if a key is present we PROBE the gateway with it; on 200 keep,
   219|	@# on anything else drop+regenerate so dashboard is always seeded with
   220|	@# a token the gateway accepts.
   221|	@API_PORT=$$(grep -E '^API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   222|	API_PORT=$${API_PORT:-8056}; \
   223|	EXISTING_KEY=$$(grep -E '^VEXA_API_KEY=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   224|	if [ -n "$$EXISTING_KEY" ]; then \
   225|		HTTP=$$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' \
   226|			-H "X-API-Key: $$EXISTING_KEY" \
   227|			"http://localhost:$$API_PORT/bots/status" 2>/dev/null || echo "000"); \
   228|		if [ "$$HTTP" = "200" ] || [ "$$HTTP" = "304" ]; then \
   229|			echo "VEXA_API_KEY in .env validates against gateway (HTTP $$HTTP) — keeping."; \
   230|			exit 0; \
   231|		fi; \
   232|		echo "VEXA_API_KEY in .env is stale (gateway returned HTTP $$HTTP) — regenerating."; \
   233|		sed -i.bak '/^VEXA_API_KEY=/d' $(ENV_FILE) && rm -f $(ENV_FILE).bak; \
   234|	fi; \
   235|	ADMIN_TK=$$(grep -E '^ADMIN_TOKEN=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   236|	ADMIN_TK=$${ADMIN_TK:-changeme}; \
   237|	USER_ID=$$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$$API_PORT/admin/users?limit=1" \
   238|		-H "X-Admin-API-Key: $$ADMIN_TK" 2>/dev/null \
   239|		| python3 -c "import sys,json; u=json.load(sys.stdin); print(u[0]['id'] if u else '')" 2>/dev/null); \
   240|	if [ -z "$$USER_ID" ]; then \
   241|		echo "No users found — creating default user..."; \
   242|		USER_ID=$$(curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$$API_PORT/admin/users" \
   243|			-H "X-Admin-API-Key: $$ADMIN_TK" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   244|			-d '{"email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","name":"Admin","max_concurrent_bots":10}' 2>/dev/null \
   245|			| python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null); \
   246|	fi; \
   247|	if [ -z "$$USER_ID" ]; then \
   248|		echo "WARNING: Could not find or create user (admin API not ready?). Dashboard WS will require login."; \
   249|	else \
   250|		TOKEN=$$(curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$$API_PORT/admin/users/$$USER_ID/tokens?scopes=bot,browser,tx&name=dashboard-ws" \
   251|			-H "X-Admin-API-Key: $$ADMIN_TK" 2>/dev/null \
   252|			| python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))" 2>/dev/null); \
   253|		if [ -n "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
   254|			echo "VEXA_API_KEY=$$TOKEN" >> $(ENV_FILE); \
   255|			echo "Created VEXA_API_KEY for user $$USER_ID (dashboard WS auth)."; \
   256|			if [ -f $(LAST_TAG_FILE) ]; then \
   257|				export IMAGE_TAG=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE)); \
   258|			else \
   259|				export IMAGE_TAG=dev; \
   260|			fi; \
   261|			$(COMPOSE_CMD) up -d dashboard; \
   262|		else \
   263|			echo "WARNING: Could not create API key for user $$USER_ID. Dashboard WS will require login."; \
   264|		fi; \
   265|	fi
   266|
   267|# --- Status ---
   268|ps: check_docker
   269|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) ps
   270|
   271|logs:
   272|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) logs -f
   273|
   274|# --- Test ---
   275|test: check_docker
   276|	@echo "Waiting for services to be ready..."
   277|	@DASH_PORT=$$(grep -E '^DASHBOARD_HOST_PORT=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "3001"); \
   278|	DASH_PORT=$${DASH_PORT:-3001}; \
   279|	for i in [REDACTED_PHONE]; do \
   280|		curl -sf "http://localhost:$$DASH_PORT/" > /dev/null 2>&1 && break; \
   281|		sleep 2; \
   282|	done
   283|	@echo "Checking services..."
   284|	@API_PORT=$$(grep -E '^API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "8056"); \
   285|	ADMIN_PORT=$$(grep -E '^ADMIN_API_PORT=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "8057"); \
   286|	DASH_PORT=$$(grep -E '^DASHBOARD_HOST_PORT=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "3001"); \
   287|	API_PORT=$${API_PORT:-8056}; ADMIN_PORT=$${ADMIN_PORT:-8057}; DASH_PORT=$${DASH_PORT:-3001}; \
   288|	if curl -sf "http://localhost:$$API_PORT/" > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   289|		echo "  ✓ API Gateway (port $$API_PORT)"; \
   290|	else \
   291|		echo "  ✗ API Gateway not responding (port $$API_PORT)"; \
   292|	fi; \
   293|	if curl -sf "http://localhost:$$ADMIN_PORT/" > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   294|		echo "  ✓ Admin API (port $$ADMIN_PORT)"; \
   295|	else \
   296|		echo "  ✗ Admin API not responding (port $$ADMIN_PORT)"; \
   297|	fi; \
   298|	if curl -sf "http://localhost:$$DASH_PORT/" > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   299|		echo "  ✓ Dashboard (port $$DASH_PORT)"; \
   300|	else \
   301|		echo "  ✗ Dashboard not responding (port $$DASH_PORT)"; \
   302|	fi; \
   303|	echo ""; \
   304|	HOST_IP=$$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $$1}'); \
   305|	if [ -n "$$HOST_IP" ] && [ "$$HOST_IP" != "127.0.0.1" ]; then \
   306|		echo "Dashboard: http://$$HOST_IP:$$DASH_PORT"; \
   307|		echo "API docs:  http://$$HOST_IP:$$API_PORT/docs"; \
   308|		echo "Admin:     http://$$HOST_IP:$$ADMIN_PORT/docs"; \
   309|	else \
   310|		echo "Dashboard: http://localhost:$$DASH_PORT"; \
   311|		echo "API docs:  http://localhost:$$API_PORT/docs"; \
   312|		echo "Admin:     http://localhost:$$ADMIN_PORT/docs"; \
   313|	fi; \
   314|	echo ""; \
   315|	echo "Image tag: $$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE) 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')"; \
   316|	echo "To test: open the dashboard and join a Google Meet."
   317|	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory test-transcription
   318|
   319|# --- Transcription test ---
   320|# Sends a real audio file to the transcription service and verifies text comes back.
   321|# Catches: wrong URL, wrong token, service down, model mismatch.
   322|TEST_AUDIO := $(ROOT)/tests3/testdata/test-speech-en.wav
   323|
   324|test-transcription:
   325|	@TX_URL=$$(grep -E '^TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   326|	TX_TOKEN=$$(grep -E '^TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); \
   327|	if [ -z "$$TX_URL" ]; then \
   328|		echo "  ✗ Transcription: TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL not set in .env"; \
   329|		exit 1; \
   330|	fi; \
   331|	if [ ! -f "$(TEST_AUDIO)" ]; then \
   332|		echo "  ✗ Transcription: test audio file not found at $(TEST_AUDIO)"; \
   333|		exit 1; \
   334|	fi; \
   335|	echo "Testing transcription at $$TX_URL ..."; \
   336|	RESP=$$(curl -s -X POST "$$TX_URL" \
   337|		-H "Authorization: Bearer *** \
   338|		-F "file=@$(TEST_AUDIO)" \
   339|		-F "language=en" \
   340|		-F "model=large-v3-turbo" \
   341|		-w "\n%{http_code}" 2>&1); \
   342|	HTTP=$$(echo "$$RESP" | tail -1); \
   343|	BODY=$$(echo "$$RESP" | head -n -1); \
   344|	if [ "$$HTTP" = "200" ]; then \
   345|		TEXT=$$(echo "$$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('text','')[:80])" 2>/dev/null); \
   346|		if [ -n "$$TEXT" ]; then \
   347|			echo "  ✓ Transcription works: \"$$TEXT\""; \
   348|		else \
   349|			echo "  ✗ Transcription returned 200 but no text"; \
   350|			exit 1; \
   351|		fi; \
   352|	elif [ "$$HTTP" = "401" ] || [ "$$HTTP" = "403" ]; then \
   353|		echo "  ✗ Transcription auth failed ($$HTTP). Check TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN in .env"; \
   354|		exit 1; \
   355|	else \
   356|		echo "  ✗ Transcription failed (HTTP $$HTTP)"; \
   357|		echo "    $$BODY" | head -3; \
   358|		exit 1; \
   359|	fi
   360|
   361|# ============================================================================
   362|# Publishing & Promotion
   363|# ============================================================================
   364|
   365|# Push all images to DockerHub with immutable tag, then tag as :dev
   366|# Uses docker tag + push (same manifest/SHA) instead of imagetools create (wraps in manifest list)
   367|publish: check_docker
   368|	@if [ ! -f $(LAST_TAG_FILE) ]; then \
   369|		echo "ERROR: No .last-tag found. Run 'make build' first."; exit 1; \
   370|	fi
   371|	@TAG=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE)); \
   372|	echo "Publishing all images with tag: $$TAG"; \
   373|	for img in $(IMAGES) $(BOT_IMAGE); do \
   374|		echo "  Pushing $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG..."; \
   375|		docker push $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG; \
   376|		echo "  Tagging $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:dev → $$TAG"; \
   377|		docker tag $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:dev; \
   378|		docker push $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:dev; \
   379|	done; \
   380|	echo ""; \
   381|	echo "Published $$TAG. :dev tags updated."
   382|
   383|# Promote a tag to :staging
   384|promote-staging: check_docker
   385|	@TAG_TO_USE="$(TAG)"; \
   386|	if [ -z "$$TAG_TO_USE" ]; then \
   387|		if [ -f $(LAST_TAG_FILE) ]; then \
   388|			TAG_TO_USE=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE)); \
   389|		else \
   390|			echo "ERROR: No TAG specified and .last-tag not found."; exit 1; \
   391|		fi; \
   392|	fi; \
   393|	echo "Promoting $$TAG_TO_USE → :staging"; \
   394|	for img in $(IMAGES) $(BOT_IMAGE); do \
   395|		echo "  $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:staging → $$TAG_TO_USE"; \
   396|		docker pull $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG_TO_USE; \
   397|		docker tag $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG_TO_USE $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:staging; \
   398|		docker push $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:staging; \
   399|	done; \
   400|	echo "Done. :staging now points to $$TAG_TO_USE"
   401|
   402|# Promote a tag to :latest
   403|promote-latest: check_docker
   404|	@TAG_TO_USE="$(TAG)"; \
   405|	if [ -z "$$TAG_TO_USE" ]; then \
   406|		if [ -f $(LAST_TAG_FILE) ]; then \
   407|			TAG_TO_USE=$$(cat $(LAST_TAG_FILE)); \
   408|		else \
   409|			echo "ERROR: No TAG specified and .last-tag not found."; exit 1; \
   410|		fi; \
   411|	fi; \
   412|	echo "Promoting $$TAG_TO_USE → :latest"; \
   413|	for img in $(IMAGES) $(BOT_IMAGE); do \
   414|		echo "  $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:latest → $$TAG_TO_USE"; \
   415|		docker pull $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG_TO_USE; \
   416|		docker tag $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:$$TAG_TO_USE $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:latest; \
   417|		docker push $(DOCKERHUB_USER)/$$img:latest; \
   418|	done; \
   419|	echo "Done. :latest now points to $$TAG_TO_USE"
   420|
   421|# ============================================================================
   422|# Database schema sync (idempotent — no Alembic, no migration versions)
   423|# ============================================================================
   424|
   425|init-db: check_docker
   426|	@set -e; \
   427|	if [ "$(REMOTE_DB)" != "true" ]; then \
   428|		if ! $(COMPOSE_CMD) ps -q postgres | grep -q .; then \
   429|			echo "ERROR: postgres not running. Run 'make up' first."; exit 1; \
   430|		fi; \
   431|		count=0; \
   432|		DB_USER=$$(grep -E '^DB_USER=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "postgres"); \
   433|		DB_NAME=$$(grep -E '^DB_NAME=' $(ENV_FILE) 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || echo "vexa"); \
   434|		DB_USER=$${DB_USER:-postgres}; DB_NAME=$${DB_NAME:-vexa}; \
   435|		while ! $(COMPOSE_CMD) exec -T postgres pg_isready -U $$DB_USER -d $$DB_NAME -q; do \
   436|			if [ $$count -ge 12 ]; then echo "ERROR: DB not ready in 60s."; exit 1; fi; \
   437|			sleep 5; count=$$((count+1)); \
   438|		done; \
   439|	fi; \
   440|	echo "Running idempotent schema sync..."; \
   441|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) exec -T meeting-api python -c "import asyncio; from admin_models.database import init_db; asyncio.run(init_db())"; \
   442|	$(COMPOSE_CMD) exec -T meeting-api python -c "import asyncio; from meeting_api.database import init_db; asyncio.run(init_db())"; \
   443|	echo "Schema sync complete."
   444|
   445|# Restore a production dump into the local postgres.
   446|# Extracts only the public schema, loads data, then runs schema sync.
   447|#   make restore-db DUMP=~/secrets/production-dump.sql
   448|restore-db: check_docker
   449|	@if [ -z "$(DUMP)" ]; then \
   450|		echo "Usage: make restore-db DUMP=<path-to-dump.sql>"; exit 1; \
   451|	fi
   452|	$(ROOT)/deploy/compose/scripts/restore-prod-dump.sh "$(DUMP)"
   453|", "total_lines": 452, "file_size": 19942, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

messages.name: read_file
messages.content:
{"content": "     1|name: vexa
     2|services:
     3|  # --- Infrastructure ---
     4|
     5|  redis:
     6|    image: redis:7.0-alpine
     7|    mem_limit: 512m
     8|    command: ["redis-server", "--appendonly", "yes", "--appendfsync", "everysec"]
     9|    healthcheck:
    10|      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
    11|      interval: 10s
    12|      timeout: 3s
    13|      retries: 3
    14|    volumes:
    15|      - redis-data:/data
    16|    networks:
    17|      - vexa
    18|    restart: unless-stopped
    19|
    20|  postgres:
    21|    image: postgres:17-alpine
    22|    # idle_in_transaction_session_timeout=60s auto-kills orphaned transactions
    23|    # (defense-in-depth against future DB session leaks in meeting-api).
    24|    command: ["postgres", "-c", "idle_in_transaction_session_timeout=60000"]
    25|    environment:

    51|      # MINIO_HOST_BIND defaults to 127.0.0.1 (safe — only the host can reach
    52|      # MinIO; presigned URLs only work for browsers running on the host).
    53|      # For external-browser audio playback (e.g. dashboard accessed from a
    54|      # remote machine), set MINIO_HOST_BIND=0.0.0.0 + set MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT
    55|      # to e.g. http://<host-public-ip>:${MINIO_HOST_PORT}. The presigned URL
    56|      # signature uses MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT; without that env, presigned URLs
    57|      # carry the in-network hostname `minio:9000` which only resolves inside
    58|      # the docker bridge network.
    59|      - "${MINIO_HOST_BIND:-127.0.0.1}:${MINIO_HOST_PORT:-9000}:9000"

Now I see the full picture. The compose stack has its own PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO. We already have these running on Swarm. Let me take the **adapt-to-Swarm** approach — use existing infra and wire Vexa in.

Actually, let me reconsider. The Vexa compose stack expects to own its services on its own Docker bridge network. Dragging it into Swarm would mean rewriting the compose file. The cleaner path for now: **standalone Docker Compose stack** on the VPS with our existing PostgreSQL, and I'll wire it behind Traefik for the dashboard + API access.

Let me check how to access our existing PostgreSQL from outside the Swarm, and set up MinIO.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Check our existing postgres service details
r = terminal("docker service inspect postgres_postgres --format '{{json .Endpoint.Spec}}' && echo '===' && docker service inspect postgres_postgres --format '{{json .Endpoint.VirtualIPs}}'")
print(r["output"])

# Also check if we can reach it from the host
r2 = terminal("docker container ls --format '{{.Names}} {{.Ports}}' | grep postgres")
print(r2["output"])

# Check postgres host networking
r3 = terminal("docker service ls --format '{{.Name}} {{.Ports}}' | grep postgres")
print(r3["output"])
"}

messages.name: execute_code

messages.content:
PostgreSQL doesn't expose a host port — it's internal to Swarm on the overlay network. I have two options:
1. Publish PostgreSQL port to host so Vexa's compose stack can reach it
2. Deploy Vexa's own PostgreSQL in its compose stack

Let me go with option 2 (simple, clean, self-contained). And deploy the MinIO + full Vexa stack as a standalone docker-compose.

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import json

# Check which Traefik network we use
r = terminal("docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep -i traefik")
print("Traefik networks:", r["output"])

# Check the existing traefik public network
r2 = terminal("docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep -i public")
print("Public networks:", r2["output"])

# Check labels used on existing stacks
r3 = terminal("docker service inspect traefik_traefik --format '{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Labels}}'")
print(r3["output"])
"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# What network is traefik listening on?
r = terminal("docker service inspect traefik_traefik --format '{{range $net, $v := .Endpoint.VirtualIPs}}{{$v.NetworkID}} {{$v.Addr}}{{\"\\n\"}}{{end}}' && echo '===' && docker network ls --format '{{.ID}} {{.Name}} {{.Driver}}' | head -20")
print(r["output"])
"}

Now I have the pattern. Traefik runs on Swarm overlay network `agent-net`. Since Vexa runs as standalone Docker Compose, I need to connect it to `agent-net` for Traefik to see it. Let me set up the proper approach: deploy Vexa's compose stack with the existing PostgreSQL host connection and network setup.

Actually, the cleanest approach for your setup: **Deploy Vexa Lite** — a single container that bundles everything (no separate PostgreSQL, MinIO, Redis containers needed). It's simpler and uses `agent-net` for Traefik.

Let me check if Vexa Lite can use an external PostgreSQL (since we have one), but actually the Lite container has Redis and everything inside. Let me just deploy compose mode with MinIO only (we skip their PostgreSQL since our existing one can be used... wait, it's on a Swarm network not accessible from standalone compose).

Let me take the simplest path: **deploy Vexa Lite with a separate MinIO container**. The Lite container has supervisord managing all services + internal Redis. We just need MinIO for recordings and point it to our Traefik network.

I need to adapt the approach. Let me deploy the **full compose stack** instead — it gives us separate containers that can join the `agent-net` network for Traefik. I'll disable their postgres/redis and point to our existing ones... wait, our existing ones are on Swarm overlay. 

**Cleanest approach**: Deploy Vexa Lite but without `--network host`. Connect it to a docker bridge network that also has MinIO, and add it to `agent-net` for Traefik.

Actually, let me think simpler. The compose stack is the right answer here — it has separate services, each can join traefik's network. Let me deploy the compose stack standalone, using their own internal PostgreSQL + MinIO (since they live on their own compose bridge network), and just expose the dashboard + API via Traefik.

function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Deploy Speaches - CPU-optimized Whisper API server
# Uses Systran/faster-whisper-medium with int8 quantization
cmd = """
docker rm -f speaches 2>/dev/null

docker run -d --name speaches \\
  --restart unless-stopped \\
  --cpus 6 \\
  --memory 8g \\
  -p 127.0.0.1:8030:8000 \\
  -v speaches-cache:/root/.cache/huggingface/hub \\
  -e WHISPER__MODEL=Systran/faster-whisper-medium \\
  -e WHISPER__COMPUTE_TYPE=int8 \\
  -e WHISPER__BEAM_SIZE=1 \\
  -e WHISPER__BEST_OF=1 \\
  -e WHISPER__VAD_FILTER=true \\
  -e ASR_ENGINE=faster_whisper \\
  -e TRANSCRIPTION_SERVER__API_KEY="" \\
  ghcr.io/speaches-ai/speaches:latest-cpu
"""

r = terminal(cmd, timeout=180)
print(r["output"])

# Check if it started
r2 = terminal("docker logs speaches 2>&1 | tail -20")
print(r2["output"])
"}

Now I need to create the .env and adapt the compose file. The compose stack has its own postgres, redis, minio. Since we can't use our Swarm PostgreSQL easily, let me use their compose but customize ports to avoid conflicts, and add it to the Traefik network.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Create the .env at repo root
env_content = """# --- Vexa Environment ---
TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8030/v1/audio/transcriptions
TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN=

# Ports - avoid conflicts with Swarm services
API_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT=8056
ADMIN_API_PORT=8057
RUNTIME_API_PORT=8090
AGENT_API_PORT=8100
CALENDAR_SERVICE_PORT=8050
DASHBOARD_HOST_PORT=3001
POSTGRES_HOST_PORT=5458
MCP_HOST_PORT=18888
MINIO_HOST_PORT=9000
MINIO_CONSOLE_HOST_PORT=9001

# Admin
ADMIN_TOKEN=vexa-admin-$(openssl rand -hex 8)

# Image tag - pre-built from DockerHub
IMAGE_TAG=latest
BROWSER_IMAGE=vexaai/vexa-bot:latest

# Database (embedded postgres)
DB_NAME=vexa
DB_USER=postgres
DB_PASSWORD: [REDACTED] Storage (embedded minio)
STORAGE_BACKEND=minio
MINIO_ENDPOINT=minio:9000
MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=vexa-access-key
MINIO_SECRET_KEY=vexa-secret-key
MINIO_BUCKET=vexa
MINIO_SECURE=false

# Make MinIO reachable from outside (for Traefik)
MINIO_HOST_BIND=0.0.0.0
MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT=http://meet.paragu-ai.com:9000
"""

r = terminal("cat > /root/vexa-deploy/vexa/.env << 'ENVEOF'\n" + env_content + "ENVEOF")
print(r["output"])
"}

Now I need to modify the compose file to:
1. Connect to `agent-net` (Traefik's network) so Traefik can reach the services
2. Add Traefik labels to the dashboard and API gateway services
3. Expose the dashboard port to `0.0.0.0` (for Traefik routing)

Let me create a custom docker-compose.override.yml instead of modifying the main file.

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"path": "/root/vexa-deploy/vexa/docker-compose.override.yml", "content": "name: vexa

services:
  # Add agent-net to all services that need Traefik visibility
  api-gateway:
    networks:
      - agent-net
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-api.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-api.rule=Host(`api.paragu-ai.com`) && PathPrefix(`/vexa`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-api.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-api.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
      - "traefik.http.services.vexa-api.loadbalancer.server.port=8000"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-api.middlewares=vexa-api-strip"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.vexa-api-strip.stripprefix.prefixes=/vexa"

  dashboard:
    networks:
      - agent-net
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-dash.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-dash.rule=Host(`meet.paragu-ai.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-dash.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.vexa-dash.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
      - "traefik.http.services.vexa-dash.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"

  # MinIO needs to be reachable for presigned URLs from browser
  minio:
    networks:
      - agent-net

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
    name: agent-net
"}

Good. But there's a problem — `speaches` won't resolve in the compose DNS because it's not part of the compose project. The Vexa containers will be on the `vexa_vexa` network. They can reach `speaches` via the `agent-net` network (it's shared), but DNS won't resolve `speaches` automatically. I need to use the IP or add a container name alias.

Let me use a different approach — skip the compose file's internal dependencies and just deploy Vexa as a set of containers connected to agent-net.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Let me take a step back and think about this differently.
# We have Swarm with Traefik on agent-net.
# Speaches is already on agent-net.
# Vexa compose creates its own bridge network.
# 
# Simplest path: Just add `external_links` or use the network alias feature.
# Actually in docker compose v3, I can make the Vexa containers use 
# agent-net as their primary network and remove the internal vexa network entirely.
# But then the compose services can't resolve each other by service name.
#
# Better: Add speaches to the compose stack as an external container.
# OR: Just use the IP address in the .env

# Let me check Speaches IP on agent-net
r = terminal("docker inspect speaches --format '{{range .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{.IPAddress}} {{end}}' && echo '---' && docker network inspect agent-net --format '{{range .Containers}}{{.Name}} {{.IPv4Address}}{{\"\\n\"}}{{end}}' | head -20")
print(r["output"])
"}

messages.name: terminal

There's the problem. Speaches is only on the internal overlay with no port mapping. Vexa containers need to reach it. Since I put Speaches on agent-net, and Vexa containers will also be on agent-net via the override, they *should* be able to reach `speaches:8000` via DNS (Docker DNS resolves container names on shared overlay networks).

But wait — standalone containers and compose containers on an overlay network may not see each other's DNS in Swarm mode. Let me take the simpler path: run Speaches with a host port AND put Vexa's transcription URL pointing to the host.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "# Re-create Speaches with host port for direct access\ndocker rm -f speaches\ndocker run -d --name speaches \\\n  --restart unless-stopped \\\n  --cpus 6 \\\n  --memory 8g \\\n  -p 127.0.0.1:8030:8000 \\\n  --network agent-net \\\n  -v speaches-cache:/root/.cache/huggingface/hub \\\n  -e WHISPER__MODEL=Systran/faster-whisper-medium \\\n  -e WHISPER__COMPUTE_TYPE=int8 \\\n  -e WHISPER__BEAM_SIZE=1 \\\n  -e WHISPER__BEST_OF=1 \\\n  -e WHISPER__VAD_FILTER=true \\\n  -e ASR_ENGINE=faster_whisper \\\n  -e TRANSCRIPTION_SERVER__API_KEY=\"\" \\\n  ghcr.io/speaches-ai/speaches:latest-cpu", "timeout": 30}

messages.content: {"output": "speaches\[REDACTED_TOKEN]db", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

Actually — the simplest reliable approach: put Speaches on `agent-net` AND add it as an external container in the Vexa override so compose containers can resolve `speaches` via DNS.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-docker-swarm", "description": "Build, deploy, and operate Next.js apps on Docker Swarm with Traefik ingress — covers image builds with private npm registries, workspace packages, Supabase key management, multi-replica deploys, and production debugging.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
